DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO PLAINTIFF EXPANXION'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. SOCIALCHORUS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, AND DENIED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS. SOCIALCHORUS HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN BY PROVIDING SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE PARTIES DID NOT ENTER INTO A WRITTEN FEE AGREEMENT FOR SERVICES ON OR ABOUT MAY 3, 2012 AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT BECAUSE THE FEE AGREEMENT WAS NOT SIGNED, AND SOCIALCHORUS HAD NO INTENTION OF SIGNING IT. (SEE DECLARATION OF SHOVE IN SUPPORT OF SOCIALCHORUS' MOTION OF SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ("SHOVE DECLARATION"), 4.) THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW THAT A TRIABLE ISSUE OF ONE OR MORE MATERIAL FACTS EXISTS AS TO THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEETS ITS BURDEN. IT DID NOT SUBMIT ANY EVIDENCE WITH ITS OPPOSITION. EVEN TAKING INTO ACCOUNT THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF IN ITS SEPARATELY FILED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE ESSENTIAL TERMS OF THE CONTRACT ALLEGED IN ITS COMPLAINT WERE AGREED UPON THROUGH THE EMAIL CORRESPONDENCE OF MAY 3, 2012. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS, SOCIALCHORUS' HAS NOT SHIFTED ITS BURDEN. SOCIALCHORUS REQUESTED INFORMATION AND SERVICES FROM EXPANXION AS TO POTENTIAL EMPLOYEES. (SEE SHOVE DECLARATION, EXH. B.) EVEN IF THE BURDEN SHIFTED, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE SERVICES SOCIALCHORUS RECEIVED FROM PLAINTIFF. SEE EVIDENCE IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' UMF 9 (HABER DECL., EX. A (MAY 3, 2012, 1:44 P.M. EMAIL FROM MR. SHOVE TO MS. KLIC STATING "OK, PLSE SKED WITH SARA[.] WE NEED ENGINEERS AS WELL.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS INC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT HEARING SET FOR AUG-06-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EXPANXION RECRUITING SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT THERE IS NO MATERIAL ISSUE OF FACT WITH RESPECT TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER HALOGEN NETWORK AND YOUCAST BECAME SOCIALCHORUS, INC. (UMF 4); AND WHETHER THE PARTIES DID NOT ENTER INTO A WRITTEN FEE AGREEMENT FOR SERVICES ON OR ABOUT MAY 3, 2012 AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT BECAUSE THE FEE AGREEMENT WAS NOT SIGNED, AND SOCIALCHORUS HAD NO INTENTION OF SIGNING IT. (UMF 10 AND SHOVE DECL. IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, 4.). PLAINTIFF DID NOT MOVE IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ITS CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS. AS SUCH THE COURT WILL NOT ADDRESS THAT CAUSE OF ACTION ON THE MERITS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.
ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Vacated

Plaintiff Expanxion Recruiting Services, A California Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL KELLY, ESQ., 415.617.2203, FOR PLAINTIFF EXPANXION RECRUITING SERVICES.
RODNEY SORENSEN, ESQ., 415.738.6841, FOR DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Plaintiff Expanxion Recruiting Services' Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. As for the first cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiff has failed to establish that there is no material issue of fact with respect to this cause of action. There is a triable issue of fact whether Halogen Network and YouCast became SocialChorus, Inc. (UMF 4); and whether the parties did not enter into a written Fee Agreement for services on or about May 3, 2012 as alleged in the Complaint because the Fee Agreement was not signed, and SocialChorus had no intention of signing it. (UMF 10 and Shove Decl. in Support of Defendant's Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment, 4.). Plaintiff did not move in the alternative for summary adjudication of its cause of action for common counts. As such the Court will not address that cause of action on the merits. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

Defendant Socialchorus Inc, Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL KELLY, ESQ., 415.617.2203, FOR PLAINTIFF EXPANXION RECRUITING SERVICES.
RODNEY SORENSEN, ESQ., 415.738.6841, FOR DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Socialchorus Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment to Plaintiff expanXion's Complaint is denied. SocialChorus' motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, and denied as to the second cause of action for common counts. SocialChorus has shifted the burden by providing sufficient evidence that the parties did not enter into a written Fee Agreement for services on or about May 3, 2012 as alleged in the Complaint because the Fee Agreement was not signed, and SocialChorus had no intention of signing it. (See Declaration of Shove in Support of SocialChorus' Motion of Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication ("Shove Declaration"), 4.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists as to that cause of action. Plaintiff has failed to meets its burden. It did not submit any evidence with its opposition. Even taking into account the evidence submitted by Plaintiff in its separately filed Motion for Summary Judgment, Plaintiff has failed to show that the essential terms of the contract alleged in its Complaint were agreed upon through the email correspondence of May 3, 2012. As to the second cause of action for common counts, SocialChorus' has not shifted its burden. SocialChorus requested information and services from expanXion as to potential employees. (See Shove Declaration, Exh. B.) Even if the burden shifted, there is a triable issue of fact as to the services SocialChorus received from plaintiff. See evidence in opposition to Defendants' UMF 9 (Haber Decl., Ex. A (May 3, 2012, 1:44 p.m. email from Mr. Shove to Ms. Klic stating "ok, plse sked with sara[.] we need engineers as well.") Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL KELLY, ESQ., 415.617.2203, APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF EXPANXION RECORDING SERVICES AND ALSO SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS, INC.; No other appearances.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION. DEFENDANT SOCIALCHORUS INC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to August 6, 2013, on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
